title: Banner Change Script Touched the Wrong Routers

ticket.summary: A Netmiko script meant to update the login banner on the lab routers (hostnames lab-rtr-*) also rewrote the banner on three production core routers during the 12:00 run. No outage, but change management flagged unauthorized production changes and wants the root cause before the script is allowed to run again.

topology.description: An inventory file lists every router in the company (production core, branch, and lab). The banner script filters the inventory by hostname to select lab devices only, then pushes 'banner login' config via Netmiko to each selected device.

logs.content:
12:00:01 selected 9 devices from inventory
12:00:04 lab-rtr-01: banner updated OK
12:00:07 lab-rtr-02: banner updated OK
...
12:00:19 core-rtr-lab1: banner updated OK
12:00:22 core-rtr-lab2: banner updated OK
12:00:25 core-rtr-lab3: banner updated OK
Jul 17 12:00:19: %PARSER-5-CFGLOG_LOGGEDCMD: user svc-netmiko logged command: banner login ...

cli_outputs.output:
targets = [d for d in inventory if "lab" in d["hostname"]]
print(f"selected {len(targets)} devices from inventory")
for device in targets:
    conn = ConnectHandler(**device)
    conn.send_config_set(BANNER_CMDS)
    conn.disconnect()

- hostname: lab-rtr-01
- hostname: lab-rtr-02
- hostname: lab-rtr-03
- hostname: lab-rtr-04
- hostname: lab-rtr-05
- hostname: lab-rtr-06
- hostname: core-rtr-lab1   # 'lab' = LABrador datacenter site code
- hostname: core-rtr-lab2
- hostname: core-rtr-lab3

monitoring.notes: Config-change audit shows 9 devices modified by svc-netmiko at 12:00 — six lab-rtr-* devices (expected) and three core-rtr-lab* production routers (unexpected). The production routers' hostnames contain the substring 'lab' because 'lab' is also the site code for the LABrador datacenter, which the script's substring filter cannot distinguish from the lab role prefix.

hints: The script selected 9 devices but only 6 lab routers exist — look at how the script decides which inventory entries are 'lab' devices.
The filter is 'if "lab" in d["hostname"]' — a substring match. Check the production hostnames it also matched.

core-rtr-lab1..3 contain the substring 'lab' (the LABrador site code), so the loose substring filter selected them. Match on the hostname prefix ('startswith("lab-rtr-")') or, better, filter on an explicit role/site field in the inventory instead of the name.

solution.root_cause: The script selects targets with a substring filter — 'if "lab" in d["hostname"]' — which matches not only the six lab-rtr-* devices but also the three production core routers core-rtr-lab1..3, whose hostnames contain 'lab' as the LABrador datacenter site code. All nine matched devices received the banner push.
solution.fix: Tighten the selection to an unambiguous criterion: hostname.startswith('lab-rtr-'), or better, filter on a dedicated role/site attribute in the inventory (e.g. role == 'lab') rather than substring-matching names; add a dry-run mode that prints the target list for approval before pushing config.
